48: Come again? A sin eater fused with a voidsent?

50: Tell me. You were investigating a ruined church of the First Light?

51: You possess a singular talent for dredging up memories I'd sooner forget.

52: ...Not that I can fault you for it. Remembrance is the burden of the living.

53: What you described sounds like a voidsent I once defeated in the Thirteenth. Back before...any of this.

54: At the time, I was just another memoriate, traveling the land and hunting the monsters that preyed upon the innocent.

55: ...Until I realized one such monster could be of use to me.

56: When I sealed it in memoria, I had no other thought but preventing its resuscitation.

57: But when I agreed to journey to the First as the Ascians' hidden emissary, I remembered that voidsent.

58: A creature of its caliber could turn the tide of battle. It could lend me the strength I needed to prevail.

59: Drawing its power from the memoria would have been a simple matter.

60: Yet after bringing it here, I left it to gather dust upon my desk in Lakeland.

61: If I truly had wanted to defeat Ardbert, that voidsent would have been my solution...

62: But I was afraid. Afraid of being twisted by Darkness like so many memoriates before me. Afraid of becoming what I hated the most.

63: Never mind that it was far too late. The Ascians' puppet, fearing corruption... Absurd, don't you think?

66: Part of you always wanted Ardbert to succeed?

68: You are kind to say so.

69: Succeed in slaying the monster, you mean? I wish I had. Would that I'd been a traitor only to myself─I could've believed my heart was in the right place, if nothing else.

70: Absurd, yet in no way amusing. I agree.

71: Above all, I was naive to think that the memoria would be safe in my former quarters, even once they were rendered inaccessible to mortal interlopers.

72: Sin eaters must have been drawn to the memoria's concentrated Darkness. They would have provided enough Light to break the seal─and suitable fodder for the fiend in its newfound freedom.

73: In sparing the First from its wrath, you have prevented untold devastation.

74: I know that words will not absolve me. Yet, as one who draws breath upon this star, I offer them nonetheless.

75: Warrior of Darkness... Nay, Warrior of Light. I thank you.
